data.title: The UK's FCA has released a final draft of its framework for crypto assets, which will bring practices such as token custody exceeding 24 hours and automated permissions under regulation.
data.description: The UK Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) has released the final draft of its crypto asset framework. The new regulations, scheduled to take effect on October 25, 2027, will bring most crypto activities under the Financial Services and Markets Act. Under the proposal, any institution holding a client's crypto assets for more than 24 hours or possessing the ability to revoke a client's rights will be considered a regulated custodian and required to hold a full guarantee license. Validators and node operators offering yield or reward reinvestment features will lose their technical exemptions. Furthermore, stablecoin issuers within the UK must control the entire lifecycle from issuance to redemption. Relevant institutions must submit authorization applications between September 30, 2026, and February 28, 2027.

data.title: Fed Governor Milan: Energy Shock Hasn't Altered Inflation Outlook
data.description: Fed Governor Milan stated that the current energy shock has not changed the inflation outlook for the next 12 to 18 months. According to Jin10, Milan noted that the inflation expectations remain similar to those before the conflict. This assessment suggests that the energy market disruptions have not significantly impacted the broader economic forecast.

data.title: Fed Governor Milan: Tariffs Not to Blame for Commodity Inflation
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Milan stated that attributing commodity inflation to tariffs is unreasonable. According to Jin10, Milan emphasized that other factors are more significant in driving the current inflationary trends in commodities. She highlighted that supply chain disruptions and increased demand are more likely contributors to the rising prices. Milan's comments come amid ongoing debates about the impact of trade policies on inflation. She urged policymakers to consider a broader range of influences when addressing inflation concerns.

data.title: South Korea pilots blockchain-based deposit tokens
data.description: According to Odaily Planet Daily, South Korea's Ministry of Strategy and Finance has selected a new pilot project for the use of blockchain digital currencies under the 2026 regulatory sandbox framework. This project will explore the use of deposit tokens to execute business promotion expenses, aiming to test the application of blockchain technology in national fiscal operations. Currently, business promotion expenses in South Korea are mainly executed through government procurement cards, and additional explanations are required for use during restricted periods such as late nights or weekends. Previously, payment methods were relatively limited due to the National Finance Management Act. This regulatory sandbox approval creates an exception for the use of deposit tokens. The ministry stated that by pre-setting conditions such as expenditure time and industry category, deposit tokens are expected to improve execution transparency and reduce the cost burden on small business owners by removing intermediaries in the payment flow. The Ministry of Strategy and Finance plans to officially launch the pilot program in the fourth quarter of 2026, with Sejong City as the first implementation site. In addition, South Korean regulators are simultaneously strengthening the supervision of stablecoins, tokenized real-world assets, and automated trading.

data.title: Adam Back advocates for an optional quantum resistance upgrade for Bitcoin.
data.description: Blockstream CEO Adam Back stated that Bitcoin should adopt an optional quantum-resistant upgrade scheme, rather than the mandatory freeze of quantum-threatened tokens proposed in BIP-361. BIP-361 proposes phasing out quantum-vulnerable addresses over five years and eventually freezing unmigrated tokens, including assets held by Satoshi Nakamoto. Recent research from Google indicates that a superconducting quantum bit system could potentially crack Bitcoin encryption in minutes with fewer than 500,000 physical qubits. Currently, approximately 6.9 million BTC are at this risk. In response to the quantum threat, BitMEX Research has proposed establishing a canary fund to implement a full freeze only when expenditures occur at relevant addresses.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Decline as Inventory Surpasses Expectations
data.description: U.S. natural gas futures have decreased, with gains narrowing to 1%. According to Jin10, the latest data from the Energy Information Administration (EIA) revealed that last week's increase in natural gas inventories exceeded expectations. This development has influenced market dynamics, leading to a reduction in the earlier gains observed in natural gas futures.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic PBC Launches Claude OPUS 4.7
data.description: Anthropic PBC has announced the release of Claude OPUS 4.7. According to Jin10, the new version, OPUS 4.7, offers fewer features compared to the Mythos preview version. The company continues to develop its AI capabilities, aiming to enhance user experience and functionality in future updates.

data.title: U.S. EIA Natural Gas Inventory Surpasses Expectations
data.description: The U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) reported that natural gas inventories increased by 59 billion cubic feet for the week ending April 10. According to Jin10, this figure exceeded market expectations of 51 billion cubic feet and was higher than the previous week's level of 50 billion cubic feet.

data.title: Snap Cuts 1,000 Jobs in AI-Driven Restructuring to Reduce Costs
data.description:
Snap has announced a significant restructuring, resulting in the reduction of approximately 1,000 jobs, which accounts for 16% of its full-time workforce. According to NS3.AI, this move is part of an AI-driven strategy aimed at reducing the company's annualized cost base by over $500 million by the latter half of 2026. The implementation of these changes is expected to incur costs ranging from $95 million to $130 million in the second quarter.

In addition to the workforce reduction, Snap has reported that its AI agents are now responsible for generating more than 65% of its new code. These AI agents also manage over 1 million queries each month, highlighting the company's increasing reliance on artificial intelligence to streamline operations and enhance efficiency.

data.title: Expert Predicts Inflation to Remain Above 3% in Coming Months
data.description: John Williams, an expert in economic trends, has forecasted that inflation will persist at levels exceeding 3% in the upcoming months. According to NS3.AI, this prediction highlights ongoing concerns about economic stability and the potential impact on consumer purchasing power. The sustained high inflation rate may influence monetary policy decisions and affect various sectors of the economy.

data.title: Paraguay Police Arrest Suspects in $1.2 Million Cyber Theft Case
data.description:
Paraguayan authorities have apprehended ten individuals, aged between 18 and 20, suspected of stealing $1.2 million from numerous bank accounts and digital wallets. According to Odaily, the police confiscated $400,000 worth of cryptocurrency, which the group allegedly used for money laundering.

The suspects reportedly recruited 400 money mules to provide personal information and bank accounts, converting stolen funds into cryptocurrency after draining victims' accounts. They purchased cryptocurrency at two to three times the market rate to expedite transactions, raising suspicions among investors who alerted the police.

Investigations revealed that the group's leader is an 18-year-old male, and multiple arrest warrants have been issued by the court.

data.title: Claude Opus 4.7 is now available.
data.description: According to official news, the latest model, Claude Opus 4.7, is now fully available. This model represents a significant improvement over Opus 4.6 in advanced software engineering, capable of handling complex long-range tasks and performing self-output verification. Opus 4.7 supports higher resolution image processing, with the longest side reaching 2576 pixels. In terms of security, the model incorporates built-in protection measures to automatically detect and block unauthorized cybersecurity requests and has launched a network verification program. Currently, Opus 4.7 is available on platforms such as API, Amazon Bedrock, and Google Cloud Vertex AI, with pricing remaining the same as Opus 4.6: $5 per million input tokens and $25 per million output tokens.

data.title: Pepe Surges 9.3% Amid ETF Filing Speculation
data.description: Pepe experienced a 9.3% increase in the past 24 hours, surpassing Bitcoin and other major tokens. According to NS3.AI, this rise in value may be linked to Canary's recent ETF filing with the SEC, despite the fund not yet receiving approval. CoinCodex analysts predict that Pepe's price could decline to $0.000002589 by April 25, 2026.

data.title: Paraguayan police seize $400,000 in cryptocurrency from a teenage hacking group.
data.description: Paraguayan police have arrested 10 suspects aged between 18 and 20 for allegedly stealing $1.2 million from hundreds of bank accounts and e-wallets. Police also seized $400,000 worth of cryptocurrency, which the group is suspected of using for money laundering. The group recruited 400 money mules who provided personal information and bank accounts, converting the funds into cryptocurrency after emptying the victims' accounts. The suspects reportedly purchased cryptocurrency at two to three times the market price to expedite transactions, a move that aroused suspicion among investors who reported them to the police. The investigation revealed that the group's leader is an 18-year-old male, and multiple arrest warrants have been issued by the court.

data.title: Hyperbridge Increases Loss Estimate from Polkadot Bridge Exploit to $2.5 Million
data.description: Hyperbridge has revised its estimated losses from a recent Polkadot bridge exploit to $2.5 million, up from an initial assessment of $237,000. According to NS3.AI, the company initially believed the theft was confined to the Ethereum bridge. However, further investigation revealed that incentive pools on Base, BNB Chain, and Arbitrum were also affected.

data.title: Australia's Digital Finance Potential Could Reach $24 Billion Annually
data.description: A report by the Digital Finance Cooperative Research Centre (DFCRC) and the Australian Digital Economy Committee highlights the significant economic potential of digital finance in Australia. According to Foresight News, the report, titled 'Unlocking Australia's $24 Billion Digital Finance Opportunity,' suggests that with appropriate regulatory advancements, the tokenization market and digital finance could contribute approximately AUD 24 billion (USD 17 billion) annually to the Australian economy.

data.title: Launch of U.S.-Regulated INJ Futures by Bitnomial
data.description: Bitnomial has introduced monthly futures contracts linked to Injective's INJ token, marking the first U.S.-regulated derivatives product for this cryptocurrency. According to NS3.AI, these contracts are now accessible to institutional clients, with plans to extend availability to retail investors via Bitnomial's Botanical platform in the coming weeks. Bitnomial stated that this launch initiates a six-month regulated trading period, potentially paving the way for a spot ETF application for INJ.

data.title: Fed's Williams Cites Uncertainty in Economic Outlook Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: Federal Reserve official John Williams expressed concerns on Thursday regarding the uncertainty surrounding the economic outlook, which limits the ability of Fed officials to provide clear guidance on future interest rate policies. According to Jin10, Williams highlighted the impact of the ongoing Middle East conflict as a contributing factor to this uncertainty. He stated, 'Given the current economic situation and the effects of the Middle East war, it is not the right time to offer explicit guidance.' Williams added that if inflation rates were to decrease to 2%, it would be appropriate to consider lowering interest rates, but emphasized that the economy has not yet reached that stage.

data.title: Spot gold and silver prices fell
data.description: According to Gate data, spot silver fell 1.00% on the day, currently trading at $78.15 per ounce. Spot gold briefly dropped $30, reaching a low of $4784.39 per ounce.

data.title: S&P 500 Index Turns Negative Indicating Weaker Market Sentiment
data.description: The S&P 500 Index has shifted to a downward trend, reflecting a weakening in market sentiment. According to ChainCatcher, this change suggests a potential shift in investor confidence and market dynamics.
